title: Release Automation, npm Trusted Publishing, and Change Control · Run and Artifact Forensics
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective multi-artifact release and publication policy; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in release automation, npm trusted publishing, and change control, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in multi-artifact release and publication policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
Publish with npm trusted publishing on supported hosted runners using exact repository, workflow, environment, id-token permission, and npm 11.5.1 or later.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Release Version Source Authority
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.

Inspect the Ci Release Version Source Authority trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Release Version Source Authority, keep Ci Release Version Source Authority connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

stimulus.title: Ci Release Version Source Authority case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A debug tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A signed tag proves the tagged source passed current required checks.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process. Artifact under review: multi-artifact release and publication policy.

options.text: The Ci Release Version Source Authority record says multi-artifact release and publication policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Ci Release Version Source Authority.

For inspect the failure record, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof. This does not satisfy Ci Release Version Source Authority for multi-artifact release and publication policy.
The record names the assumption, applies Ci Release Version Source Authority, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Release Approval Separation Duty
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-release-approval-separation-duty-fxs1dh". Tailor its release-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Release Approval Separation Duty.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Release Approval Separation Duty into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, keep Ci Release Approval Separation Duty connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-release-approval-separation-duty-fxs1dh
  - competency: ci-release-approval-separation-duty
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: release approval separation duty
    environment: sandbox Google Cloud project with a private Cloud Run service
    fault-injection: return a transient registry error after the image push begins
    retained-proof: runner image runtime version authority network and teardown record
    case-key: fxs1dh
    change: |
      source: protected-reviewed-tag
      checks: required-at-source-sha
      build: once
      approval: promote-existing-artifact
      image: immutable-digest
      npm: trusted-publishing
      npm-minimum: 11.5.1
      node-minimum: 22.14.0
      id-token: release-job-only
      package-provenance: verified
      separation: author-reviewer-builder-approver-publisher
    verify: compare tag authority source checks artifact digest package contents trusted publisher identity provenance approval and published result
    failure: Reject tag-only authorization, per-environment rebuild, long-lived publish token, OIDC for private install, provenance as benign-code proof, or approval count as safety.
    repair: Protect source authority, build once, approve promotion, publish through exact OIDC identity, verify package provenance, and separate duties by risk.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Ci Release Approval Separation Duty structural check is only one gate. multi-artifact release and publication policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Release Approval Separation Duty precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Release Approval Separation Duty, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: More manual approval steps always improve release safety.
